programme.title: Location, Location, Location Australia
programme.desc: This week Veronica and Bryce are heading to Canberra, to help two couples trying to get a leg up on the property ladder. Gab and Monique are making the biggest move of their lives, with three kids and a dog in tow, they're swapping Alice Springs for the nation's capital. But big moves don't come without big wish lists, and things don't always go according to plan. Meanwhile, long distance lovers Tarun and Mel have spent the last three years apart, now they want to buy their first home together. The budget is tight, but that's not stopping them from having big dreams, and it's Bryce and Veronica's job to find the middle ground!

programme.title: Selling Houses Australia
programme.desc: Terri and Paul are selling a main house and guest house on a large waterfront block on Lake Macquarie, north of Sydney. They're asking $1.2 million, but have been four years on the market with three agents, so something isn't working. It's very dated and multi-coloured and in need of a face lift to meet the market. The couple need to move on as they're keen to go caravanning while Terri still can (he has early-onset dementia).

programme.title: Selling Houses Australia
programme.desc: Former infantryman Ben bought this modest weatherboard in New Norfolk, northwest of Hobart, in 2008, confident it was an idyllic place to raise his family but when his wife Claire's father died suddenly in 2010, they decided to move back to the mainland closer to family and quickly bought in Victoria. They put New Norfolk on the market where it's remained on/off for five years, bringing in a modest rent. With four children, Ben is struggling with the two mortgages and works most weekends, missing out on the kids' sports etc. With a new agent on the case, it's time to upgrade New Norfolk and offload it once and for all.

Among those working within the court system is newly appointed Judge Lola Carmichael, a highly regarded and impressive deputy district attorney who doesn't intend to sit back on the bench in her new role, but instead leans in, immediately pushing the boundaries and challenging the expectations of what a judge can be. Those offering Lola counsel are her roguish best friend and colleague, Mark Callan, a successful deputy district attorney who now must adjust to the shifting boundaries in their personal and professional relationship; Supervisory Judge Lisa Benner, an influential, powerful figure tasked with overseeing Lola's transition into her new job; and Lola's efficient, pragmatic judicial assistant, Sherri Kansky.

programme.title: Blue Bloods

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: The outback is one of the world's last wild frontiers and, for the giant trucks who roam this epic, harsh country, there's only one way to get from A to B - the hard way. Whether it's taking on scorching deserts, or battling torrential rains that turn roads into rivers, this is a land that can destroy in a heartbeat. For the men and women who risk their rigs and their lives to face it, this punishing wilderness is a home away from home. We hit the road train again for lots of dirt and dangers, joining some of the toughest, most skilled truckers on the planet, as they haul their massive loads through hell on Earth. Without them, Australia would grind to a halt. Their mission: to get their freight in and their trucks out. In this episode, Jared and Johnny's epic mission grinds to a halt, Roscoe battles the elements on a world-famous rally, and Turbo is flagged down by the police.

programme.title: Truck Night In America
programme.desc: This program makes history with the toughest truck obstacle course ever built. America and trucks go together and, since 1925 when the first American factory-produced truck rolled off the line, American owners have been devising ingenious ways to make them faster, stronger, and better. Five truck owners will compete in a series of gruelling challenges that test their craftsmanship and driving ability to the max. Each episode features unique challenges that test trucks in a way that's never been seen before. The challenges culminate in The Green Hell, a three-mile obstacle course which includes challenges that send trucks flying off jumps, over a mountain of crushed cars and even through a snake-infested swamp. Helping them along the way are four experts in the off-road world - they coach, they build, they fix, but the one thing they can't do is drive. In the end, the truck who finishes is crowned the champion - if they can make it that far. In this episode, it's time for the Big Dogs: five trucks with 40-inch-plus tyres roll onto the course. But big rigs mean big damage. In the Green Hell, a rivalry heats up with a collision into a tree and a finalist lost in the woods.

programme.title: Aussie Pickers
programme.desc: Join Lucas Callaghan and Adam McDonald, Australia's own pickers, as they scour the country's back roads and barnyards in the quest to turn one man's trash into another man's treasure. Along the way we meet some of Australia's most authentic and colourful characters, whose stories reveal a rich Australian history, and what the pickers find will surprise and astound you.

programme.title: Spectacular Spider-Man
programme.desc: An animated television show that focuses on a sixteen-year-old Peter Parker, and the origins of Spider-Man.

programme.title: Adventure Time: Fionna & Cake
programme.desc: Stranded in a desolate land, Fionna, Cake and Simon are stranded and resort to desperate measures to escape.

programme.title: Adventure Time: Fionna & Cake
programme.desc: Simon finds his mind transported across time and space while danger closes in on Fionna and Cake.

programme.title: Shipping Wars
programme.desc: Follow independent shippers who have discovered that fortunes can be made by transporting items that traditional carriers won't touch. Each episode of Shipping Wars dives into the cutthroat world of these heavy-duty movers as they battle for the chance to transport the un-shippable. The competition begins with uShip, the world's largest online auction house for independent truckers. From oversized loads and bulky packages to the truly bizarre, these shippers will haul anything if the price is right. From rookie haulers to seasoned veterans, no shipment is too big for these professionals to handle. Marc, Jarrett, Jennifer, Roy and his beloved cat Muffy are back, along with new cast members Chris and Robbie. One of the industry's most unique shippers, Chris's edge in the business comes from his truck, which runs off of a waste vegetable oil system that he invented himself. While he's on the road, his girlfriend and business partner Robbie books loads and maps out routes while taking care of her son, Carter. Don't let their age fool you, this South Carolina couple juggles parenthood, running a successful business, and taking care of their golden retriever, Gix, who is a permanent passenger on the road.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Take a look inside the doors of a family-run pawnshop in Las Vegas, where three generations of men from the Harrison family, grandfather Richard, son Rick and grandson Corey jointly run the business, with clashing and camaraderie every step of the way. This family lives and breathes the pawning business, using their sharp-eyed skills to carefully assess the value of items their colourful customers bring in, objects ranging from the obscure to the truly historic.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Take a look inside the doors of a family-run pawnshop in Las Vegas, where three generations of men from the Harrison family, grandfather Richard, son Rick and grandson Corey jointly run the business, with clashing and camaraderie every step of the way. This family lives and breathes the pawning business, using their sharp-eyed skills to carefully assess the value of items their colourful customers bring in, objects ranging from the obscure to the truly historic.

programme.title: Shipping Wars
programme.desc: Follow independent shippers who have discovered that fortunes can be made by transporting items that traditional carriers won't touch. Each episode of Shipping Wars dives into the cutthroat world of these heavy-duty movers as they battle for the chance to transport the un-shippable. The competition begins with uShip, the world's largest online auction house for independent truckers. From oversized loads and bulky packages to the truly bizarre, these shippers will haul anything if the price is right. From rookie haulers to seasoned veterans, no shipment is too big for these professionals to handle. Marc, Jarrett, Jennifer, Roy and his beloved cat Muffy are back, along with new cast members Chris and Robbie. One of the industry's most unique shippers, Chris's edge in the business comes from his truck, which runs off of a waste vegetable oil system that he invented himself. While he's on the road, his girlfriend and business partner Robbie books loads and maps out routes while taking care of her son, Carter. Don't let their age fool you, this South Carolina couple juggles parenthood, running a successful business, and taking care of their golden retriever, Gix, who is a permanent passenger on the road.

programme.title: Alone
programme.desc: In order to endure the Arctic for 100 days, the participants start building shelters that can make it for the long haul. They turn their attention to procuring food, drawing the attention of dangerous predators.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Sludge takes on foreign roads and strange loads in the land of the kiwi. Turbo's new truck gets busted and bashed, on a tour of destruction. And Carl Andrews has more wheels, more weight, and more worry, than one trucker can take.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: The outback is one of the world's last wild frontiers and, for the giant trucks who roam this epic, harsh country, there's only one way to get from A to B - the hard way. Whether it's taking on scorching deserts, or battling torrential rains that turn roads into rivers, this is a land that can destroy in a heartbeat. For the men and women who risk their rigs and their lives to face it, this punishing wilderness is a home away from home. We hit the road train again for lots of dirt and dangers, joining some of the toughest, most skilled truckers on the planet, as they haul their massive loads through hell on Earth. Without them, Australia would grind to a halt. Their mission: to get their freight in and their trucks out. In this episode, Carl Andrews gets behind the wheel to transport a sizeable load, Russell's mobile classroom is shaken, and the Ferrises manage to dodge raging bush fires.

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: Mike uncovers a mind-blowing collection of vintage baseball cards in Kansas while Robbie and Jersey try to broker a deal in Tommy's 'black hole of stuff'.

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpsons writer's room is the stuff of TV legend. It would churn out hilarious scripts like a Ford automobile assembly line, turning what could have been a niche, off-beat miss into a surprise hit. The phenomenal success of the show has almost always been attributed to the irreverent and groundbreaking writing, but while the writers toiled away, tensions rose among the three creators.

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Just as critical to the success of the show as the writers, the animators had drama and tension of their own. The funky, punk rock animation house Klasky Csupo, led by Gabor Csupo had secured the contract of a lifetime, but could they hold on to The Simpsons? Through internal strife and natural disasters, the animators gave The Simpsons their signature look.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: The outback is one of the world's last wild frontiers and, for the giant trucks who roam this epic, harsh country, there's only one way to get from A to B - the hard way. Whether it's taking on scorching deserts, or battling torrential rains that turn roads into rivers, this is a land that can destroy in a heartbeat. For the men and women who risk their rigs and their lives to face it, this punishing wilderness is a home away from home. We hit the road train again for lots of dirt and dangers, joining some of the toughest, most skilled truckers on the planet, as they haul their massive loads through hell on Earth. Without them, Australia would grind to a halt. Their mission: to get their freight in and their trucks out. In this episode, Mark Cromwell begins to feel the pressure as he works to complete a job worth $2 million, and Turbo faces trouble as his load reaches the verge of destruction.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Aisha Tyler hosts another hilarious episode of the legendary improv show featuring regulars Wayne Brady, Ryan Stiles and Colin Mochrie, along with guest performer Jeff Davis and star guest TV presenter and former boxing champion Laila Ali. In a succession of brilliant games, the team provide weird medical exams and disastrous job interviews in Scenes from a Hat, present a stream of inventive suggestions for some bizarre props, and Wayne and Jeff tackle the subject of acne in the style of mariachi and Les Miserables in Greatest Hits. Laila Ali also joins in the fun, acting out a modelling photo shoot scenario with Wayne providing her voice and learning to box with her grizzled trainer Ryan in Helping Hands.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Aisha Tyler hosts another hilarious episode of the legendary improv show featuring regulars Wayne Brady, Ryan Stiles and Colin Mochrie, along with guest performer Jeff Davis and special guests Mary Killman and Mariya Koroleva from the US Olympic synchronised swimming team. There's a mixture of brilliant games - both old and new - for the performers to get stuck into: Hollywood Director Colin coaches the others through a criminal break-in scene, a seductive housewife tempts a hunky electrician in the ingenious Sideways Scene, and an eventful snowboarding session tests the performers' memories and wits to the full in the always challenging Forward Rewind. Special guests Mary and Mariya from the US Olympic synchronised swimming team also get to join in the fun, acting as a variety of props in a dense jungle adventure scene and being serenaded by boy banders Jeff and Wayne.

programme.title: In Treatment
programme.desc: A high-powered CEO with anxiety issues turns to Paul for therapy at the encouragement of his wife.

programme.title: In Treatment
programme.desc: Paul resumes his Friday sessions with Gina, who agrees to give a deposition in the lawsuit against him while also helping him through the new changes in his life.

programme.title: Girls
programme.desc: Hannah, Adam and Jessa each try to convince Caroline and Laird to forgo their planned home birth while Shoshanna faces a crucial decision when presented with a unique job opportunity. Meanwhlie, after Marnie and Desi's meeting with a record label exec, Ray lets Desi know what he really thinks of him.

programme.title: Girls
programme.desc: At Marnie's upstate wedding, Hannah brings Fran along as her date. A visiting Shoshanna is tasked with carrying out Marnie's wishes, and a dejected Ray steps up.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: We meet our anti-hero Ray, as well as his daughter, Brittany, ex-wife, Jacinta, unwell brother, Bruce, best friend, Gary, and boss, Freddy. Freddy owes Ray money which he doesn't have, as usual so he sends Ray off with newly hired heavy, Nick, to collect and keep a 10k debt. Nick hasn't collected before and proves ineffective, so Ray has to step in and do it his way to get results. Ray meets Ally at the dog park and there is immediate chemistry - but he doesn't act on it, feeling that she's out of his league. Gary is in trouble with his Russian wife, Tatiana, over the discovery of a porn DVD and asks Ray to take the blame. While out with Brittany having ice-cream they bump into some young men who knock her ice-cream to the ground and disrespect Ray. Ray assaults one of the young men responsible and while escaping the scene he runs into Ally and takes the opportunity to make his feelings a little clearer. Ally gives Ray her number.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: Ray kidnaps a man named Luke Henson and "takes care" of him out in a forest. Brittany questions her dad about Santa Claus and unicorns. The police have identified Ray as the young man's assailant; he informs Bruce that he has to go to court. Ray has his first date with Ally. It goes well. Freddy informs Ray that he was mistaken about Luke Henson. Ray is not happy about having killed the guy. Showing some moral compass and compassion Ray decides to pay Luke's widow an anonymous visit and give her some cash. Gary is in an induced coma after being bashed in a gun deal gone wrong set up by his brother-in-law, Vasilli. Vasilli's story doesn't add up and when confronted with a surprise visit by Ray, some of the truth comes out. Once Ray discovers who Gary's assailants were, he lets them know that they shouldn't mess with the people he cares about.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: Gary wakes from his coma, and thanks Ray for doing "a bit of this and a bit of that" to Vassili and the guys who bashed him. Ray and Nick are becoming friends and he asks Nick for a favour. Jacinta berates Ray for telling Brittany that Jesus isn't real. The court has ordered Ray to attend anger management. His first meeting doesn't go very well. Freddy informs Ray that the guys he bashed for putting Gary in a coma belong to a crew run by his son-in-law's brother, Davros. Freddy insists Ray sits down with Davros to smooth the waters. Nick makes good on the favour. While on drugs, Vasilli breaks into Gary's house to rob the contents of his safe. Gary, who has just returned home from hospital, manages to send an SOS text to Ray who is out shopping with Ally. Ray comes to Gary's rescue and promptly deals with Vasilli - but they'll have to work out what to do with the body as Tatiana has arrived home unexpectedly.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: Gary takes Ray for a drive to give him a surprise as a thank you for saving him. Ray and Ally's relationship has developed. Freddy takes Ray to meet Davros. Ray doesn't like Davros and although Davros seems placated by the end of the conference, Freddy knows better. A road-rage incident forces Ally to confront Ray about his quick access to violence, rather than attempting to diffuse a situation. When court-ordered anger management therapist Peter confronts Ray about the same impulse, Ray explains that it's justifiable to let people clearly know that there are ramifications for being an a******. For Ray, it's all about respect. Ray brings Ally home to meet Brittany. Ray can't sleep and gets up to notice a van parked outside surveilling his house. He goes for a drive and leads the van out to a safe place to do some permanent damage to it's occupants - Davros' guys - using the surprise Gary gave him.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.desc: Ray and Gary are planning to relieve a guy of his drugs and cash, and bring Nick in on the job, but a sudden turn of events scuppers their plans. After informing Freddy about his and Gary's plans going astray, Ray is picked up by Davros' slow-witted bodyguard, Bobby, and the wily hitman, Dave, who abduct Ray in his own car. Ray tells his captors about a hidden strongbox containing a great deal more cash than they're being paid to do the job. Dave devises a plan: tell Davros they ran out of gas, tell Ray that they'll let him go once the strongbox is retrieved, retrieve it, deliver Ray to Davros, and get paid twice. What could possibly go wrong? Dave and Bobby uncover the strongbox, but when they momentarily leave Ray's car unattended with Ray safely locked in the trunk, the car disappears. Dave and Bobby hot wire another car and go looking for Ray, the car, and the strongbox.

programme.title: Mr Inbetween
programme.sub-title: Your Mum's Got A Strong Box
programme.desc: We meet potheads, Scon and Lefty, and discover they are the reason for the disappearance of Ray and his car. Scon and Lefty drop off Ray's car to auto mechanic, Bernie, to be chopped for parts. Bernie is surprised to find Ray in the boot and has to dump Ray's car in a nearby forest with him still tied-up inside. Ray kicks his way out of the trunk, and shortly afterwards encounters Scon and Lefty with his phone and his strongbox. Trailing them back to Bernie's auto shop, Ray confronts the trio just as they're about to torch open his strongbox, which Ray reclaims intact. Dave and Bobby intercept Ray, and finally deliver him to Davros. As Ray prepares to be dispatched to his final resting place, Davros decides he wants in on the contents of the strongbox. Only when it's finally opened do Davros, Dave, and Bobby discover that it doesn't exactly contain what they were expecting, and are reminded that greed isn't necessarily good.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: It is two years earlier: in Camp Toccoa, GA, volunteers train for America's new military experiment, the paratroops. Theirs is one of nine companies in the 506th Parachute Infantry Regiment: E, aka Easy Company, commanded by the domineering Lt. Herbert Sobel. Through a regimen that involves frequent six-mile climbs up and down Currahee, a local mountain, Sobel succeeds in drilling the men into an elite unit. But his heavy-handed tactics, punishments for petty or imagined infractions, and strategic failures during manoeuvres, alienate Sobel from his men, who prefer his cool-headed second-in-command, Lt. Winters. After the 506th is shipped to England to complete training, Sobel harasses Winters by citing him for failing to inspect a latrine on time. When Winters requests a trial by court-martial, six of his sergeants: Salty Harris, Chuck Grant, Bill Guarnere, Carwood Lipton, Mike Ranney and Floyd Talbert request transfer rather than follow Sobel into combat.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Landing in a field, Winters finds he has lost his rifle and supply bag. He teams up with a private from another company, John Hall, and sets out to find their units. During the night they link up with a few more soldiers, and ambush a horse-drawn German supply convoy, their first enemy encounter. Regrouping in a nearby town, Winters runs into Easy's Lt. 'Buck' Compton, who tells him that Lt. Meehan is unaccounted for, which puts Winters in charge. His orders are to lead an attack on a nearby cluster of German artillery pieces endangering the seaborne infantry at Utah Beach. Winters deploys a small group on the entrenched enemy positions, and eventually takes four artillery pieces in succession, disabling them with TNT. The euphoria is tempered, however, when Winters finds Hall dead, killed by machine-gun fire. The mission is successful, but Winters has lost his first man as acting company commander.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Lt. Harry Welsh delivers Easy's next orders: take Carentan, the only town where armoured vehicles from Omaha and Utah beaches can link up. Winters briefs the men, and Easy heads into Carentan, encountering fierce resistance from Germans holed up in buildings. The town is eventually secured, although Winters gets hit above his ankle by ricochet gunfire.

programme.sub-title: Lan Mao Shi Zai Wuding Shang
programme.desc: Walden's past day or so with Kate has him convinced she is the one he will spend the rest of his life with, until a stranger knocks on his door.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: An Expensive Glitch And A Goof-Off Room
programme.desc: Due to a four-hour gap between his classes, Sheldon is given his private dorm room. Running into fellow student Sam, she says he gets undeserved special treatment, and nobody likes him because of his arrogance. Talking with Dr. Sturgis about humans being social creatures, Sheldon decides to experiment to see if people will like him more. After letting Sam use the room to study, word spreads Sheldon has a free room. He allows couples to use it to study, unaware of why they want it. When his parents find out, they tell him people are using it to waste time, convincing Sheldon to stop lending it out. Meanwhile, a glitch in the slot machines lets June win $11,000. Unable to pay it, Connie is forced to let June become a partner in her casino business. June and Georgie support each other's ideas for the place. Connie tries to talk Georgie to her side, but he says Connie hates anything she doesn't think of herself. Georgie adds a small disco ball, further angering Connie.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: A Lock-In, A Weather Girl And A Disgusting Habit
programme.desc: Mary has Sheldon and Missy attend an overnight church lock-in. With Billy also attending, George feels nervous that he could meet Brenda without anyone knowing. Mary also tries to avoid being alone with Pastor Rob while chaperoning the event. George takes Principal Petersen out and runs into Brenda anyway. George awkwardly leaves. Mary and Pastor Rob bond while smoking cigarettes outside. George sees them together and leaves without Mary knowing he was there. Missy tricks Sheldon into hiding in a closet, and he falls asleep there. Georgie meets a woman, Mandy, (Emily Osment), at the laundromat, who says she is 25. Claiming he is 21, he takes her on a date, which goes well. When Mandy reveals she is actually 29, Georgie keeps quiet about his own age.

Mary succeeds, but later has a guilt-ridden dream for doing so, and quits being a seller. Sheldon is on Spring Break from college when he hears about a three-day comic convention. Unable to get an adult to go with him, Sheldon decides to rebel and take a bus to the convention. Seeing someone from his local comic shop and his old science teacher Mr. Givens, they debate the merits of Lost in Space, Star Trek and The Next Generation. Sheldon enjoys the trip though gets in trouble afterwards for sneaking out.

programme.desc: Rebecca and Sam are trapped overnight in Robin Colcord's deluxe security apartment while the gang passes the time playing Monopoly.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Frederick is coming to spend Christmas with his dad, and Frasier has ordered some educational toys for him. When the gifts are sent to Maine by accident, Frasier makes a dash to the mall, to find replacements.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.sub-title: It's Hard To Say Goodbye If You Won't Leave
programme.desc: Just as Frasier is about to tell Kate how he feels about her, she reveals she's moving to another city. At the last minute, they test the possibility of a relationship.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.sub-title: Last Of The Really Odd Lovers
programme.desc: Grace hates herself for her being attracted to Nathan and wants to keep her embarrassing romance with him a secret. Meanwhile, Jack revels in the attention from Grace's eccentric friend, Val.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Grace tries to mediate between a resentful Will and her sloppy, free-spirited boyfriend Nathan when Nathan moves in.

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.sub-title: Heat 7 - Joe Sugg, Daryl McCormack, Lenny Rush
programme.desc: Presenters Joan Bakewell and Stephen Mangan return for the tenth series of Portrait Artist Of The Year. In a special opening episode to celebrate the 10th anniversary, eight previous winners return to paint an exceptional sitter, Dame Judi Dench in a battle to become Portrait Artist Of The Decade.

programme.title: Project Greenlight: A New Generation
programme.desc: A docuseries focusing on the next generation of talented female filmmakers who are given the chance to direct a feature film. Issa Rae will appear in every episode as an Executive Producer, providing guidance and mentorship to these aspiring filmmakers.

programme.title: Steve Jobs: The Lost Interview
programme.desc: In this candid, in-depth interview with the late visionary filmed in 1995, Steve Jobs discusses at length his early days, career battles, and vision for the future. Small portions of the piece were used for a television series at the time, but the vast majority was shelved and for 17 years thought to be lost. Resurfacing, it is being presented in its entirety, providing a fascinating look at Jobs at a particularly interesting moment in his career, two years before he would go on to retake control of Apple.

programme.title: My Name Is Gulpilil
programme.desc: "I'm an actor, I'm a dancer, I'm a singer and also, a painter. This film is about me. This is my story of my story". David Gulpilil is an iconic figure of Australian cinema and has been for fifty years. His mesmerising, electrifying presence has leapt off the big screen and changed Australian screen representation forever.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo visits Essex to discover why dairy herds travelled there by rail from all over the country in the 19th century. He also visits Waltham Cross to see how the gunpowder made there fuelled the building of an empire and heads to Hackney to uncover the gruesome details of the first murder on a train.

programme.title: Great Film Composers
programme.desc: In the mid-80s, films like Back to the Future, Die Hard and The Abyss dominated cinema, and four composers took centre stage.

programme.title: Cursed Treasures
programme.desc: With the help of archaeologists and scientists, this series looks to uncover the truth behind the deadly curses bestowed on the finders of some of the world's most fascinating treasures, shining a light on Ancient History and playing into people's thirst for conspiracy theories.

programme.title: Landscape Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Hundreds of artists from all over the UK and Ireland have battled with brushes, rollers, paints and pencils for just 3 places in the final. As the sun sets over London, our three finalists face their toughest challenge yet to create a night-painting of this iconic landmark that will see judges Tai Shan Schierenberg, Kate Bryan and Kathleen Soriano crown one of them the Landscape Artist of the Year 2019.

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year

programme.title: Comedy Legends
programme.desc: Barry Cryer celebrates the career of the Carry On, Doctor in the House and The Navy Lark star.

programme.title: Great Film Composers
programme.desc: In the mid-80s, films like Back to the Future, Die Hard and The Abyss dominated cinema, and four composers took centre stage.

programme.title: Project Greenlight: A New Generation
programme.desc: A docuseries focusing on the next generation of talented female filmmakers who are given the chance to direct a feature film. Issa Rae will appear in every episode as an Executive Producer, providing guidance and mentorship to these aspiring filmmakers.

programme.title: The Wonder List with Bill Weir
programme.desc: Conservationists turn killers in this battle to turn back time by eradicating mammal invaders threatening the islands' unique ecosystem.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: A look at the Britpop band's most famous videos, including Parklife, featuring Phil Daniels, Country House, directed by Damien Hirst, and Coffee & TV.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: Human League singer Phil Oakey and director Steve Barron reflect on the iconic videos of the new wave band, including that of hit single Don't You Want Me.

programme.title: Other Voices
programme.desc: Mumford & Sons is a British folk rock band formed in London in 2007; Hozier is, as the song goes, a man you don't meet every day.

programme.title: Other Voices
programme.desc: Rufus McGarrigle Wainwright is an American-Canadian musician. John Grant established himself as one of the great musicalchroniclers of the American Dream, angled mostly from its flipside.

programme.title: Canada's National Parks
programme.desc: Canada is the second largest country in the world and historically the first to recognize the need to protect the diversity of its landscapes and preserve them for future generations. The over 100-year-old concept of Canadian National Parks is instrumental in protecting the unique natural environment and habitats of native flora and fauna from human influence. This is a challenging task for the employees of Parks Canada, as they have to reconcile tourism and economic interests with nature. And then, of course, there is climate change. Whether and how these developments are putting a strain on sensitive ecosystems will be the main topic of the discovery series, which brings Canada's unique landscape and culture to life. Knowledgeable and charismatic protagonists, including scientists, First Nations people, environmental activists, artists and storytellers who have a special connection with the landscape guide the viewer through this journey.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Follow Michael Portillo as he experiences the thrill of global rail travel, visiting locations recommended by his historic guidebook, and uncovering stories from the past.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Historian Paul Murton returns for more fascinating tours of Scotland. With Victorian guidebook Black's Picturesque Guide to Scotland in his hands, Paul follows in the footsteps of the first tourists to Scotland. Travelling by age-old modes of transport, including a horse-drawn caravan and steam train, Paul explores the most fascinating parts of the country that have charmed visitors since the birth of Scottish tourism 200 years ago.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Historian Paul Murton returns for more fascinating tours of Scotland. With Victorian guidebook Black's Picturesque Guide to Scotland in his hands, Paul follows in the footsteps of the first tourists to Scotland. Travelling by age-old modes of transport, including a horse-drawn caravan and steam train, Paul explores the most fascinating parts of the country that have charmed visitors since the birth of Scottish tourism 200 years ago.

programme.title: Gone Before Her Time
programme.desc: A celebration of four female singers whose lives were tragically cut short - Amy Winehouse, Aaliyah, Lisa Lopes and Selena Quintanilla-Perez.

programme.title: Love To Love You, Donna Summer
programme.desc: Shaped by Summer's own reflections, the memories of close family, friends and colleagues, and filled with the sounds of Summer's songs, this film is an in-depth look at the iconic artist as she created the music that took her from the avant-garde music scene in Germany, to the glitter and bright lights of dance clubs in New York, to worldwide acclaim. Her voice and artistry became the defining soundtrack of an era. A deeply personal portrait of Summer on and off the stage, the film features a wealth of photographs and never-before-seen home video footage - often shot by Summer herself - and provides a rich window into the surprising range of her artistry, from songwriting to painting, while exploring the highs and lows of a life lived on the global stage.

programme.title: Other Voices
programme.desc: Mumford & Sons is a British folk rock band formed in London in 2007; Hozier is, as the song goes, a man you don't meet every day.

A deeply personal portrait of Summer on and off the stage, the film features a wealth of photographs and never-before-seen home video footage - often shot by Summer herself - and provides a rich window into the surprising range of her artistry, from songwriting to painting, while exploring the highs and lows of a life lived on the global stage.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: A look at the Britpop band's most famous videos, including Parklife, featuring Phil Daniels, Country House, directed by Damien Hirst, and Coffee & TV.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: Human League singer Phil Oakey and director Steve Barron reflect on the iconic videos of the new wave band, including that of hit single Don't You Want Me.

programme.title: Other Voices
programme.desc: Mumford & Sons is a British folk rock band formed in London in 2007; Hozier is, as the song goes, a man you don't meet every day.

programme.title: Other Voices
programme.desc: Rufus McGarrigle Wainwright is an American-Canadian musician. John Grant established himself as one of the great musicalchroniclers of the American Dream, angled mostly from its flipside.

programme.title: Canada's National Parks
programme.desc: Canada is the second largest country in the world and historically the first to recognize the need to protect the diversity of its landscapes and preserve them for future generations. The over 100-year-old concept of Canadian National Parks is instrumental in protecting the unique natural environment and habitats of native flora and fauna from human influence. This is a challenging task for the employees of Parks Canada, as they have to reconcile tourism and economic interests with nature. And then, of course, there is climate change. Whether and how these developments are putting a strain on sensitive ecosystems will be the main topic of the discovery series, which brings Canada's unique landscape and culture to life. Knowledgeable and charismatic protagonists, including scientists, First Nations people, environmental activists, artists and storytellers who have a special connection with the landscape guide the viewer through this journey.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Follow Michael Portillo as he experiences the thrill of global rail travel, visiting locations recommended by his historic guidebook, and uncovering stories from the past.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Historian Paul Murton returns for more fascinating tours of Scotland. With Victorian guidebook Black's Picturesque Guide to Scotland in his hands, Paul follows in the footsteps of the first tourists to Scotland. Travelling by age-old modes of transport, including a horse-drawn caravan and steam train, Paul explores the most fascinating parts of the country that have charmed visitors since the birth of Scottish tourism 200 years ago.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands

programme.title: Italy's Great Lakes
programme.desc: The lakes of Italy's far north are known for their enchanting allure. This series explores the five largest of them.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: Gary Kemp, Simon Le Bon and director Russell Mulcahy are among those reflecting on the electronic music scene and music video boom of the 1980s.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: Directors discuss working with Aretha Franklin, including the difficulties experienced making the video for her duet with George Michael.

programme.title: Other Voices
programme.desc: James Morrison's 2006 debut single You Give Me Something became a hit worldwide; Kodaline are an Irish rock band originally known as 21 Demands.

programme.title: Other Voices
programme.desc: Ryan Adams has been roundly recognised as one of the most talented songwriters of his generation; Glen Hansard is an Irish singer-songwriter.

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael looks at his second travel series, Pole to Pole (1992), and how it came about.

programme.title: The Jinx: Life & Deaths Of Robert Durst
programme.desc: A dismembered corpse is found floating in trash bags in Galveston Bay, Texas. Police trace the body back to a grisly crime scene, and arrest Robert Durst for the murder of his neighbour, Morris Black. Durst, scion of New York's billionaire real estate family, makes bail and goes on the run. After months of eluding authorities, he is arrested again and assembles a dream team of lawyers to prepare his defence in the extraordinary case.

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Presenters Joan Bakewell and Stephen Mangan return for the tenth series of Portrait Artist Of The Year. In a special opening episode to celebrate the 10th anniversary, eight previous winners return to paint an exceptional sitter, Dame Judi Dench in a battle to become Portrait Artist Of The Decade.

programme.title: The World According To Amazon
programme.desc: An exploration into Amazon's business models and practices, from its inception to present day.

programme.title: Food, Inc. America's Food Industry
programme.desc: An unflattering look inside America's corporate controlled food industry. The current method of raw food production is largely a response to the growth of the fast food industry since the 1950s. The production of food overall has more drastically changed since that time than the several thousand years prior.

programme.title: Secrets of Penthouse
programme.desc: In the 1980s, Penthouse is at its height as one of the most successful magazines in America. Following the release of Bob Guccione's infamous Roman porno epic "Caligula," Penthouse faces a moral backlash from both the religious right and feminist left. Always keen to push boundaries, Guccione publishes nude pictures of the first black Miss America, Vanessa Williams, triggering a public outcry while sending sales skyrocketing.

programme.desc: Nina (Lauren Velez) is forced to intervene when Eddie (Michael DeLorenzo) lets his emotions get the best of him while trying to stop a murderous gun lord's killing spree. With Malik Yoba.

programme.title: Chicago P.D.
programme.desc: When Atwater goes undercover in a dangerous South Side drug syndicate, he learns there may be more to its kingpin than meets the eye. Halstead tries to join Atwater undercover, but finds himself at a disadvantage. Voight petitions the brass to show Ruzek leniency in his court case.

programme.title: Chicago Fire
programme.desc: Herrmann goes to court to testify against Freddie but when the perpetrators father reaches out to beg forgiveness for his sons malicious attack, a tormented Herrmann is left to figure out what to do. Following the aftermath of the recent tornado, Lt. Casey learns the makeshift shelter housing victims is on the brink of shutting down.

programme.title: New York Undercover
programme.desc: J.C. (Malik Yoba) investigates the bombings of churches in Harlem; a failing friendship feeds G's (George Gore II) growing prejudice against whites. With Michael DeLorenzo and Jonathan LaPaglia.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit
programme.desc: As the investigation into a sex-for-housing scheme expands, Benson's loyalty to Garland is tested.

programme.title: Law & Order: Special Victims Unit

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace is in Cornwall at an enormous bakery where they produce 180,000 Cornish pasties a day. Meanwhile, Cherry Healey is delving into the science of the onion.

programme.title: The Great Australian Bake Off
programme.desc: In the signature challenge, the bakers make mouth-watering individual puddings, Maggie hands down a technical challenge of her dried apricot pavlova, and the bakers face a spectacular tiered showstopper.

programme.title: Prue Leith: My Cotswold Kitchen
programme.desc: Gardener Sarah Raven cooks up a stunning dish in the Cotswold Kitchen. Meanwhile, Prue demonstrates a foolproof, easy hack for making the perfect vinaigrette salad dressing, every time.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The new batch of bakers elevate a loaf cake, tackle a Technical with a twist, and make bamboozling illusion cakes. Whose cake fakery will earn them the first Star Baker award?

programme.title: Great British Bake Off: Festive Specials
programme.desc: New Year's Special with past contestants Chigs, Lottie, Manon and Antony.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Home Cooking
programme.desc: Gordon's ultimate guide to home cooking continues, as he teaches us how to cook for friends. For dinner, Gordon's son, Jack, helps him cook roasted chicken with chickpea stuffing and a fabulous hazelnut meringue tower.

programme.title: Jamie's Quick & Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie is cooking up 4 brilliantly useful, outrageously tasty dishes. Smoky pancetta cod- the perfect fish dish when you're in a hurry, followed by a super-simple asian inspired snack that packs a serious punch: asian fried eggs. Next up it's a timesaving family favourite, crispy garlicky chicken; and to top it all off, Jamie's ridiculously tasty mango rice pudding. If you think you haven't got time to cook, think again.

programme.title: Rachel Khoo: Little Paris Kitchen
programme.desc: Rachel serves a quiche Lorraine with melt-in-the-mouth pastry. She fulfils an all-time desire by turning her hand to crepe-making with a Parisian street vendor to produce the city's most famous and favourite snack.

programme.title: Christmas Breaks With Gregg Wallace
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace is on the ultimate Christmas adventure - a magical journey through Lapland in the Arctic Circle, the most festive place on earth and home to Santa Claus.

programme.title: Jamie's One-Pan Christmas
programme.desc: Jamie kicks off Christmas with a range of delicious dishes to see us through the festive season. From crispy duck and noodles to short rib beef, veggie filo pie to decadent dessert.

programme.title: Inside The Factory Christmas Special
programme.desc: Chocolates are an essential part of many people's festive celebrations. In this Christmas special, Gregg Wallace visits a factory that produces a staggering 2 million tins of festive chocolate assortments a year.

programme.title: The Great Canadian Bake Off
programme.desc: Christmas Special with past contestants Colin, Jodi, Sachin and Linda.

programme.title: James Martin's Christmas Comforts
programme.desc: James takes a trip down memory lane and re-creates festive feasts inspired by his childhood. Special guest is racing driver Mark Webber.

programme.title: Eat Well For Less
programme.desc: Can Chris and Jordan help a convenience-loving family to plan more, cook one meal for all and save some of their hard-earned cash?

programme.title: Jamie's One-Pan Christmas
programme.desc: Jamie kicks off Christmas with a range of delicious dishes to see us through the festive season. From crispy duck and noodles to short rib beef, veggie filo pie to decadent dessert.

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: Lancashire cake make Molly, turns to the silver screen to bring a touch of glamour to a 100th birthday celebration. Rosie also takes a trip to the movies for her stunning Bollywood Dancer cake. Containing 13 kilos of chocolate orange cake and 30 kilos of fondant, the masterpiece takes a whooping 6 days to make. Eloise heads west with a Canadian Teepee Wedding Cake. Standing 3 feet high, she creates a unique animal hide style fondant tent that will go around the cake, covered in hand painted native North American designs.

programme.title: Inside The Factory Christmas Special
programme.desc: Chocolates are an essential part of many people's festive celebrations. In this Christmas special, Gregg Wallace visits a factory that produces a staggering 2 million tins of festive chocolate assortments a year.

programme.title: Masterchef: The Professionals Rematch
programme.desc: Four of the most memorable chefs from past series return to show how far their food and careers have progressed.

programme.title: The Great Australian Bake Off
programme.desc: In the signature challenge, the bakers make mouth-watering individual puddings, Maggie hands down a technical challenge of her dried apricot pavlova, and the bakers face a spectacular tiered showstopper.

programme.title: Junior Bake Off
programme.desc: It's day two of finals week and the remaining bakers are on a mission to impress. They must fuse two delicious street food items in the technical before creating supersize snacks.

programme.title: Jeremy Pang's Asian Kitchen
programme.desc: All the dishes have a bit of spice, even the pudding. Jeremy starts with mouth-watering Sichuanese chicken, a warming dish using Sichuan peppercorns to create a unique numbing.

programme.title: Justine Schofield Queensland Gourmet
programme.desc: Justine heads to a local turmeric farm and forages for ingredients, and Terri Taylor prepares a batch of turmeric chicken skewers. Up in Port Douglas, Justine cooks up some kangaroo.

programme.title: Justine Schofield Queensland Gourmet
programme.desc: Justine visits the Spanish hideaway in the heart of Queensland, Paronella Park, and makes paella. Back on Whitehaven Beach, she grills up some mango cheeks.

programme.title: Farm to Fork
programme.desc: Michael will be making his crackling pork belly. Courtney will be making her parsnip and cardamom cake with a healthy twist! Courtney and Andy will then head out to the broccoli farm.

programme.title: Farm to Fork
programme.desc: Andy teams up with Darren Robertson to create a delectable dish of fried eggs and greens. Courtney prepares her flavourful brussels sprout slaw, accompanied by a Green Goddess tahini yogurt dressing.

programme.title: Paddock To Plate
programme.desc: It's 7th week on the road Matt travels to the country music capital in Australia, Tamworth. This region known as the New England North West and has a special place in his heart because this is where he was born. Matt spent his first five years living in this region, more on that later. His first day in the area and Matt meets up with Doug Cush a wheat farmer who specialises in durum wheat the key ingredient in pasta. Doug flies Matt over the plains and fields of crop from Narrabri airport to his home in Bellata - a place world famous for its produce that even Italian past making companies knows about it. And Matt soon finds out that for the last 25 years he's been making his pasta all wrong. The next day he heads back towards Tamworth to the Dungowen Valley to find a chef with a difference. Graham Manvell and his wife Marlene show Matt their zoo of unique farm animals all bred with the purpose of going on the plates of their restaurants. But their most unique animal is the herd of Asian & Italian Water Buffalo that roam the valley. This captures Matt's attention and he cooks an old favourite with a buffalo twist - Buffalo Wellington. Not far from the Buffalo farm is Matt's next stop - Peel Valley Milk. Owner & dairy farmer Malcolm is a rebel in the dairy world deciding to build and run his own dairy factory to get his milk straight from the paddock to the hands and mouths of consumers. This is milk like it used to taste and it brings back memories for Matt's about his early years on his family dairy farm. Matt decides to track down his old family farm in Currububula. He left when he was five years old but the memories come flooding back as he drives back up the road to Glenbrooke Farm. An emotional Matt meets the current owner Karen Arnott and some old family friends who shed some light on him as a child. Matt's been asked to cook up a festive feast for the Tamworth council social club so he goes in search of the best Turkeys he can find in the region.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The new batch of bakers elevate a loaf cake, tackle a Technical with a twist, and make bamboozling illusion cakes. Whose cake fakery will earn them the first Star Baker award?

programme.title: Jamie: Together at Christmas
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver gets the festivities started with a sublime Christmas party menu of bramble cocktails, a stunning beef bourguignon topped with chestnut and bacon sprinkle.

programme.title: Jamie's One-Pan Christmas
programme.desc: Jamie kicks off Christmas with a range of delicious dishes to see us through the festive season. From crispy duck and noodles to short rib beef, veggie filo pie to decadent dessert.

programme.title: Jamie's One Pan Wonders
programme.desc: Jamie shares more fabulous one-pan dishes: foolproof tray-baked salmon in a bag, super-easy tomato fritters that one can rattle out in a frying pan, and roasted rolled pork and risotto.

programme.title: Tom Kerridge's Sunday Lunch
programme.desc: Tom shares his speedy method for the perfect roast lamb with salsa verde and minty potatoes. For dessert, he makes an apricot pie with almond crumble.

programme.title: Inside The Factory Christmas Special
programme.desc: Explore the fascinating factory processes and surprising history behind the UK's favourite festive treats. Gregg Wallace visits the world's largest mince pie factory, and discovers the challenge of producing 2,000 perfect pies every minute. Cherry Healey travels to South Wales, where factory workers make enough tinsel in a year to reach from their hometown to Hawaii. And Ruth Goodman finds out about the explosive history of Christmas crackers.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick Stein's journey of discovery brings him to the sunnier climes of the Mediterranean, where he explores the French-Catalan legacy of Roussilon on the foothills of the Pyrenees next to the Spanish border. This is the land that celebrates the best of mountain cuisine with its grilled sausages and snails together with the finest fish straight out of the region's famous self-contained sea lakes. What unites them is the local love for Aioli, the pungent garlic mayo, cherished in southern France. In his country kitchen, Rick cooks a hearty dish of pork and beef meatballs in a tomato and piment sauce.

programme.title: James Martin's Home Comforts
programme.desc: James encourages everyone to experiment in the kitchen and makes amazing comfort food from everyday larder ingredients. He is joined by special guest Cyrus Todiwala.

programme.title: James Martin's Islands To Highlands
programme.desc: James returns home to his native Yorkshire, where he is joined by good friend and multi-award-winning chef Brian Turner.

programme.title: Mary Berry's Highland Christmas
programme.desc: Dame Mary Berry travels to her mother's homeland of Scotland for a magical winter break. She is joined by friends Andy Murray, Iain Stirling and Emeli Sande.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: For the last leg of their route 66 odyssey, the bikers are going west to California, the golden state. Si and Dave have been invited by one of the youngest executive chefs in the country to learn how to make the best mashed potato in the world.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The new batch of bakers elevate a loaf cake, tackle a Technical with a twist, and make bamboozling illusion cakes. Whose cake fakery will earn them the first Star Baker award?

programme.title: High Country Farm
programme.desc: It's lambing season on Royalburn Station and they're coming thick and fast. Nadia and Carlos get to see just how much their increased flock has produced and how that affects their bottom line.

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Together
programme.desc: Jamie uses one wonderful piece of steak, big enough for six people, to ensure extra flavour and ease of cooking. He serves it with beautifully crispy, garlicky potatoes that are twice cooked for extra crunch.

programme.title: Jamie's Christmas Cookbook
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver has been cooking Christmas for his family for 20 years. In that time, he's experimented with loads of different ways of cooking the classics. But this year, he wants to show us his ultimate recipes - the ones he's decided really are the very best for Christmas. These are the dishes he'll be cooking on the big day for his own family this year.

programme.title: Beautiful Baking With Juliet Sear
programme.desc: Juliet Sear creates some stunning centrepieces. There's a fabulous multi-coloured pinata cake, a cheesy risotto cake and a stunning vegetable tart.

programme.title: High Country Farm
programme.desc: It's lambing season on Royalburn Station and they're coming thick and fast. Nadia and Carlos get to see just how much their increased flock has produced and how that affects their bottom line.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers: Route 66
programme.desc: For the last leg of their route 66 odyssey, the bikers are going west to California, the golden state. Si and Dave have been invited by one of the youngest executive chefs in the country to learn how to make the best mashed potato in the world.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Secret France
programme.desc: Rick Stein's journey of discovery brings him to the sunnier climes of the Mediterranean, where he explores the French-Catalan legacy of Roussilon on the foothills of the Pyrenees next to the Spanish border. This is the land that celebrates the best of mountain cuisine with its grilled sausages and snails together with the finest fish straight out of the region's famous self-contained sea lakes. What unites them is the local love for Aioli, the pungent garlic mayo, cherished in southern France. In his country kitchen, Rick cooks a hearty dish of pork and beef meatballs in a tomato and piment sauce.

programme.desc: Jamie uses one wonderful piece of steak, big enough for six people, to ensure extra flavour and ease of cooking. He serves it with beautifully crispy, garlicky potatoes that are twice cooked for extra crunch.

But this year, he wants to show us his ultimate recipes - the ones he's decided really are the very best for Christmas. programme.title: Ainsley's Market Menu
programme.desc: Ainsley is headed up north to the Global Food Market in Logan to experience one of the most culturally diverse markets. The challenge is on to turn this bounty of global produce into a market menu.

programme.title: High Country Farm
programme.desc: It's lambing season on Royalburn Station and they're coming thick and fast. Nadia and Carlos get to see just how much their increased flock has produced and how that affects their bottom line.

programme.title: River Cottage Christmas
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and the River Cottage team serve up an extra special festive menu with a twist this Christmas. Hugh is ready to celebrate Christmas with a renewed sense of the value of meat - but also the wonders of seasonal vegetables. This year all the meat comes from the most extensively-living, free-ranging animals of them all - wild game - and more specifically those that are viewed by many people as pests. The River Cottage chefs sharpen their knives in pursuit of wild boar, muntjack deer and greylag geese. Head gardener Mark Diacono and Hugh seek to make the most of their veg patch, even in the depths of winter, unearthing some Christmas favourites, as well as some heritage varieties from off the beaten path. Forager John Wright teams up with Pam 'the Jam' Corbin to make some luxury stocking fillers. And the Canteen hosts a '12 Tasters of Christmas' night, packed with ideas to create an array of canapes and little bites, perfect for that festive drinks party.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The new batch of bakers elevate a loaf cake, tackle a Technical with a twist, and make bamboozling illusion cakes. Whose cake fakery will earn them the first Star Baker award?

programme.title: Great British Bake Off: An Extra Slice
programme.desc: Jo Brand joins Alison Hammond and Aisling Bea to celebrate cake week with unseen footage and a new batch of bakers. Tom Allen reviews audience bakes.

programme.title: Great British Bake Off: Festive Specials
programme.desc: Paul and Prue celebrate with the Baker Ghosts of Christmas Past, featuring bakers from each year of Bake Off. The festive special ends with a performance by the Citizens of the World Choir.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace is in France at an enormous foundry where they produce a cast iron pot every five seconds. He follows production of casserole dishes, from the arrival of 20 tonnes of crude iron right through to brightly coloured orange pots. Along the way he tests his mettle by taking a sample of molten iron at 1,550 degree Celsius. With only a heatproof visor and gloves as protection he dips a ladle in to a bubbling cauldron and pours the white-hot sample into a tiny mould. He also discovers that the coloured enamel they protect their pots with is made from glass.

programme.title: The Great Australian Bake Off

programme.title: Jamie's Quick & Easy Food

programme.desc: Jamie shows how to make the most succulent turkey ever, including gravy, simple stuffing, roasting with festive butter, how to make carving simple, and the best way to use your leftovers.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace is in Wigan, at an enormous soup factory where they produce 2 million tins a day. He follows the production of vegetable soup, from a pea harvest in Yorkshire right through to the finished soup going into cans and being dispatched. Along the way he watches as a 5 tonne avalanche of peas, around 7 million individual peas, are frozen within 2 hours of being picked. He mixes up 3 tonnes of veg and 500 gallons of tomato sauce and watches as they're combined and packed into 10 000 tins. He's astonished by a 27.4 metre tall pressure cooking tower and surprised to learn that for every 1 degree drop in temperature in winter the factory observe a 3.5 percent increase in soup sales.

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace is in Ireland, at an enormous liqueurs factory where they produce 540,000 bottles a day. He follows the production of cream liqueur, from the arrival of maize to make Irish Whiskey, right through to dispatch of the finished liqueur. It's our longest ever production timeline, taking more than 3 years. Along the way Gregg learns that it's the barrels whiskey is matured in that create around half of its flavour and discovers that a milk protein is the secret to mixing cream and whiskey together.

programme.desc: Christmas just wouldn't be the same without cakes, and as the decorations go up, 6 of our Extreme Cake Makers are hard at work pushing their creations to new seasonal sugary highs! Ben has a battle to create a 3-foot tall Naughty Elf Cake in time to delight revellers and dignitaries at Chester's Christmas Lights Parade. Hand carved from chocolate cake and sugar paste, it weighs in at a staggering 30 kilos. Molly must tackle not one but three animal firsts, as she sculpts a lemur, meerkat, and armadillo, in the hope of creating the perfect festive bake to celebrate Santa's arrival at an Exotic Zoo. Suzanne flexes her sugar crafting and story writing skills as she creates Father Christmas reading a gingerbread book, covered in 3D modelling chocolate figures, that tells the tale of how Santa got his first red coat.

programme.desc: As Costello works her job at a local peep show, a journalist offers an exciting opportunity. Gloria unexpectedly forms a connection at the funeral home, while Selby meanders back to his old ways.

programme.title: Rain Dogs
programme.desc: Costello goes on an underwhelming date, while Iris confides in Gloria. After an old acquaintance from prison demands restitution, Selby comes to Costello with a proposition.

programme.title: Rain Dogs
programme.desc: In Bruton, Costello is finally able to hold a part-time job and live her best life. As cracks in their family unit begin to form, their seemingly perfect new life begins to deteriorate, with devastating results.

programme.title: Rain Dogs
programme.desc: When a visit with her family drudges up old demons, Costello finds the courage to say goodbye to Selby. Allegra arrives to help Selby clean up his act. Gloria receives surprising news.

programme.title: Rain Dogs
programme.desc: As he pines for a phone call from Costello, Selby acclimates to his new reality. Costello has found a safe space to land, but must continue to hustle to make a living.

programme.title: Rain Dogs
programme.desc: Post-visit from Fen, Selby enters a heated conversation with his therapist. After receiving surprising news that may turn around her future, Costello ties up some loose ends before leaving London.

programme.title: Rain Dogs
programme.desc: After receiving a troubling call from Iris, Selby rounds up Gloria and Lenny for a road trip in search of Costello, who is close to her breaking point after receiving upsetting news.

programme.title: Enlightened
programme.desc: Amy Jellicoe is a 40-year-old woman who returns home to California after a month's stay at a holistic treatment facility, a result of having a mental breakdown at work triggered by her self-destructive ways. Amy returns to her old life with a new cultivated approach and perspective, which includes daily meditation and exhorting the power of self-help and inner healing. Though Amy wants to be an 'agent of change' in the world, the people who know her best are sceptical of her latest intentions. She moves in temporarily with her somewhat-estranged mother, Helen and reconnects with her ex-husband Levi, who is struggling with his own demons and addictions. While trying to heal Levi and mend her relationship with Helen, Amy also re-enters work at Abaddonn Industries.

programme.title: Enlightened
programme.desc: Amy's hopes to land a dream job take a hit when she's demoted to the basement of Abaddonn, working in data entry among a group of social misfits. The job has its perks, however, as Amy uncovers a litany of corporate improprieties while surfing the net in her downtime. Meanwhile, Amy's attempts to improve the lives of her ex-husband Levi and mum Helen fall on deaf ears.

programme.title: Enlightened
programme.desc: Jealous of Krista's good fortune and fed up with her humiliation at Abaddonn, Amy considers leaving the company for a socially responsible job at a homeless shelter. But the low pay, combined with a huge unpaid bill from the treatment centre, end up giving her pause.

programme.title: Enlightened
programme.desc: Wary that she may revert to her old, hard-partying ways on her first weekend back at home, Amy talks Levi into taking a rafting excursion to the Kern River, which they used to frequent during happier times when they were married. The idyllic trip takes a downturn however when Amy discovers that her mission to implement change in Levi may be more difficult than she realised.

programme.title: Enlightened
programme.desc: After learning on TV about the deportation woes of a Mexican-born mother, Amy tries to motivate her co-workers to join her at a lunchtime rally for the family. Later, with the support of Tyler, Amy decides to organise a women's group at Abaddonn, and looks to enlist Judy, head of Human Resources, by taking her out to dinner. She also targets Krista's baby shower as a place to recruit members, but learns her cause and passion aren't shared by the suburban domestic set.

programme.title: Enlightened
programme.desc: Amy is thrilled when Sandy, whom she'd met in Hawaii during group therapy, comes to visit her for a couple of days. The kindred relationship between the two starts eroding when Helen refuses to put up Sandy for another night, forcing Amy to drop her friend off with Levi. When Sandy fails to show up to teach a lunchtime yoga session at Abaddonn, Amy's baser instincts get the better of her.

programme.title: Enlightened
programme.desc: A disturbing dream leads Amy to confront the emptiness in her life and her lingering feelings for Levi. At work, after Dougie gets a promotion, Amy looks to get into his good graces by setting him up with Harper, a pretty co-worker of Krista's who had attended the yoga session. The incident brings her closer to Tyler, who has his own issues with loneliness.

programme.title: Enlightened
programme.desc: Friction increases between Amy, Dougie and the staff when she finds herself reported to HR for job deficiencies. Desperate to move out of the department, Amy tries to get Krista to get her a meeting with Damon, with predictable results.

programme.title: Enlightened
programme.desc: Amy goes off to work, leaving Helen left alone with her thoughts and memories - including those of her late husband, Jim. At the grocery store, she runs into old friend Carol, whose husband used to work with Jim, and endures the prerequisite show-off pictures of children and grandkids. Later, Levi stops by to drop off a photo album, and ends up venting about Helen's role in his breakup with Amy.

programme.title: Enlightened
programme.desc: After Levi goes ballistic in the middle of the night, Amy convinces him to follow in her footsteps and get treatment at Open Air. At work, a reinvigorated Amy talks Damon into letting her present her research findings to his group. The problem is that Dougie, who no longer talks to Amy without a 'witness', won't let her leave the office. No matter - Amy makes her presentation anyway and pays the price, both with Damon's team and with Dougie. Bristling but still battling, Amy turns to Tyler for the key to forcing change, finally, at Abaddonn.

programme.title: Enlightened
programme.desc: Looking for "the key" to ruin Abaddonn and "free" its employees, Amy orchestrates a meeting with a charming whistle-blowing journalist, Jeff Flender, sharing some of the incriminating emails she's printed out at work with Tyler's IT password. Though he doesn't find a story in the emails, Jeff encourages Amy to dig deeper and find more explosive evidence that could help bring down the company.

programme.title: Enlightened
programme.desc: Amy and a still-reluctant Tyler meet Jeff at an LA jazz club, where Jeff reveals he's already been working on a story about Abaddonn but needs proof of bribery - leading Amy to revel in her revenge fantasies. When several top execs can't log onto their accounts, Abaddonn's breach of security is exposed - leaving Tyler scrambling to find a solution that won't lead investigators to him or Amy. Suspecting foul play, Dougie vows to root out the true perps, while Krista is rushed to the hospital with a pregnancy complication.

programme.title: Enlightened
programme.desc: Amy receives a letter from Hawaii from Levi, who has been bristling under the constraints of Open Air, its holistic philosophy, and his bodily-function-obsessed roommate Tony. To cope, Levi escapes with a pair of fellow malcontents, Travis and Danielle, for a night of drugs, alcohol and debauchery at a nearby hotel. After pushing himself to his hard-partying limits, Levi decides he's ready to return to Open Air and give rehab one more chance.

programme.title: Enlightened
programme.desc: Amy becomes inspired to use social media to spread her message of change, and attends a gathering of liberal intellectuals on Jeff's invitation. Dougie learns who hacked into Abaddonn's system, but has second thoughts about reporting the breach to HR. Amy has a chance run-in with Abaddonn CEO Charles Szidon.

programme.title: Enlightened
programme.desc: In an effort to dig up dirt on Szidon, Amy, Tyler and Dougie (still irate over being betrayed by management) take turns trying to befriend the CEO's assistant, Eileen. Eventually agreeing to join the trio for drinks after work, Eileen takes a shine to Tyler, who ends up with a guilty conscience when the two start dating.

programme.title: Enlightened
programme.desc: In the giddy afterglow of finding "gold" in Szidon's personal emails, Amy lets down her guard with Jeff, and indulges herself in daydreams of building a perfect life with the dashing journalist. The dream comes to an abrupt end, however, when Levi unexpectedly returns from rehab in Hawaii.

programme.title: Enlightened
programme.desc: In hopes she will drop her crusade against Abaddonn, Tyler gets Eileen to arrange a meeting for Amy with Szidon, who turns out to have more compassion than Amy expected. Dougie goes off the rails after getting official word about Cogentiva's future. Amy confides in Krista about the Abaddonn expose, and has a tense meeting with Levi.

programme.title: Enlightened
programme.desc: On the precipice of bringing down Abaddonn, Amy warns Tyler to prepare himself for the inevitable fallout. She also tells her mum about the expose, but Helen's reaction is not what Amy had anticipated. When Szidon gets wind of her intentions, Amy wrongly points a finger at Krista, who has just given birth. As the crisis reaches its denouement, Amy is intercepted by HR, who take her to meet with Szidon and Abaddonn lawyers for a final reckoning.

programme.title: Rain Dogs
programme.desc: As he pines for a phone call from Costello, Selby acclimates to his new reality. Costello has found a safe space to land, but must continue to hustle to make a living.

programme.title: Rain Dogs
programme.desc: Post-visit from Fen, Selby enters a heated conversation with his therapist. After receiving surprising news that may turn around her future, Costello ties up some loose ends before leaving London.

programme.title: Rain Dogs
programme.desc: After receiving a troubling call from Iris, Selby rounds up Gloria and Lenny for a road trip in search of Costello, who is close to her breaking point after receiving upsetting news.
